data: Giuseppe Nenci, «I decreti da Entella I-V», Annali della Scuola Normale Superiore di Pisa, Classe di Lettere e Filosofia ser.3, 21, fasc. 1 (1991): 137–45
Entella 1 (Nenci) = C2 (Ampolo): friendship and alliance with the Herbitaioi

Lines 1-3 are centred in the pediment. From line 4 onwards, the text is situated on the main rectangular body of the tablet. Lines 5-22 maintain a left-hand margin of one letter-space; line 4 begins hard against the left border fo the tablet. Line 23 (final line) is centred with larger letters (approximately double size).
First Punic War (264-241 BCE), and commonly attributed to the second half of the war.
Image courtesy of the Centre for the Study of Ancient Documents, Faculty of Classics, University of Oxford (tablet pre-cleaning, c.1977)

entella 1 (nenci) = c2 (ampolo): friendship and alliance with the herbitaioi
Text encoding: Jonathan Prag, edition source: #JP
Entella 2 = C3: friendship and alliance with the Geloans

Lines 1-3 are centred in the tympanon of the tablet. From line 4, the text is sited on the main rectangular body of the tablet. The left margin is ‘justified’, the right is not. The letters of line 27 are slightly taller and much wider and more widely spaced than the rest.
Daux, 1984, Notes de lecture. I. Le dossier d’Entella (Sicile): sept documents authentiques, plus un faux moderne
entella 2 = c3: friendship and alliance with the geloans
Entella 3 = Nakone A: decree of reconciliation

The text fills the face of the tablet. Lines 1-3 are indented by c.2 letters, and line 2 is surely complete and ended with a vacat occupied by the now lost nail hole upper right. Lines 34 and 35 appear compressed (implying the engraver was aware when he began line 34 that the text would run over; the vacat of line 35 is only partial, as line 34 falls towards the lower edge as it proceeds).

The several instances in which the cutter omitted one or more letters and subsequently inserted them above the already engraved text are notable (ll.8, 13, 20, 33); the phenomenon is not to be seen on the other tablets.
Image courtesy of the Centre for the Study of Ancient Documents, Faculty of Classics, University of Oxford
Asheri, 1984, Il decreto di Nakone (SEG, XXX, nr. 1119): Addenda et Corrigenda

entella 3 = nakone a: decree of reconciliation
Entella 4 = B1, honours for Tiberius Claudius Antiatas

Entire text is sited on the main rectangular body of the tablet. The tympanon is occupied by a depiction of a triple-plumed helmet that intrudes down into lines 1-3, which it divides. The text maintains a left-hand margin of c.1 letter space, the left margin being ‘justified’, the right not. The empty space after the text is equivalent to 6 or 7 lines of text.
Ampolo, 2001, Da un'antica città di Sicilia. I decreti di Entella e Nakone
Souza, 2024, Entella: a resilient ancient Sicilian community
entella 4 = b1, honours for tiberius claudius antiatas
Entella 5 = A1, Entella thanks those who helped in the synoikismos

Lines 1-5 are roughly centred in the tympanon of the tablet. From line 6, the text is sited on the main rectangular body of the tablet. The left hand margin is ‘justified’, the right not. Line 15 exceeds the left hand margin by one letter space; the implication of both the position of the initial kappa and its form would seem to be that the engraver initially omitted it.
Manganaro, 1990, Metoikismos-metaphora di poleis in Sicilia: il caso dei Geloi di Phintias e la relativa documentazione epigrafica
Lombardo, 1997, Il decreto V di Entella: note di lettura
entella 5 = a1, entella thanks those who helped in the synoikismos
